body_txt: Bartenders don't have it easy. They have to deal with lowlifes, drunks, soldiers and some of the clientele with their better armed arsenal , and the average Joe sometimes confesses his life problems to them , especially if he has drunk too much and his personality emerges or he's having a particularly bad day . The Bartender Confidant may return the favor with some advice. Just how valuable this advice might be depends on the story, but when you're desperate and maybe a bit drunk, asking for it might seem like a good idea. Others may just act as a sounding board, a listening ear or a shoulder to cry on. This way the trope serves as way for a character to voice their inner struggles or conflicts with those close to them. Expect him to hear " I'll Tell You When I've Had Enough! " pretty often. This trope may be exploited by the Intel-Gathering Bartender , as alcohol tends to loosen lips, especially in fantasy or medieval settings, where taverns are not only the local gathering spot, but also where travelers visiting the town may stay. This logically makes the local bartender the person to ask about what's going on in town. They may take up the duty of a quest givers in any fantasy (or science-fantasy )-themed Tabletop Game . They may also be undercover spies, gathering information from tipsy officials for the benefit of La Résistance or a foreign state. A subtrope of The Confidant and Amateur Therapist . Compare with Obsessive-Compulsive Barkeeping or Flair Bartending . Sister Trope to Chatty Hairdresser , where a barber or hairdresser is the one acting as a confidant and/or dispensing life advice. May overlap with Secret-Keeper if they are the sort to not share what's been told to them. May also be a source of Simple-Minded Wisdom .
